title: Every state-mutating HTTP endpoint must declare its error contract enumerating all failure modes a client can observe
statement: For every endpoint with HTTP method in `{POST, PATCH, PUT, DELETE}` in `.devai/state/sensors/inventory_api/api-map.json#/endpoints[*]`, the endpoint MUST declare an error contract enumerating every distinct error response the caller can observe, keyed by `{statusCode, errorCode, retriability}`. The contract lives in one of: (a) a sibling `*.errors.json` file under `docs/framework/contracts/`, (b) a `@ApiResponse({status, type})` decorator chain on the controller method that produces a discoverable shape, or (c) implicitly via a use-case `alternateFlows[]` line documenting the failure path. The minimum enumerated error set MUST include: validation failures (4xx), authorization failures (401/403), resource-not-found (404 for endpoints with path params), conflict (409 where idempotency keys are honored), and rate-limit-exceeded (429 where the endpoint is rate-limit-bound). Endpoints without an enumerated error contract are a release-gating finding under this invariant. Read endpoints (GET) are exempt — error contracts for reads are a future INV-ERROR-002 candidate.
rationale: Stynx's `StynxErrorFilter` (in `@stynx/core`) translates exceptions into stable error response shapes, and adopters get consistent error envelopes for free — but pre-Phase-30 no invariant required adopters to *document* which exceptions each endpoint can throw. `sense-spec-robustness-targets` (Phase 27.E) flagged stynx FAIL on F1×T8 because zero invariants were typed `error_semantics`. This invariant promotes a baseline gate scoped to mutations (write endpoints have the highest impact when a client can't predict failure modes — silent retries can corrupt state). Adopters who use NestJS's `@ApiResponse` decorators get most of this for free; the gate forces the documentation discipline. Conservative scope (mutations only) keeps the initial promotion tractable — read-endpoint error contracts can land as INV-ERROR-002 if/when needed.
